element_roles.rationale: The object translates along the x axis with a non-zero range, which strongly indicates a lever-style slider used to lower and raise the toast carriage.
The object rotates around the z axis with a 270 degree range, which is clear evidence of a user-adjustable rotary control.
Its name and front-panel placement indicate a pressable function-selection button.
Its name clearly indicates a pressable cancel/stop control on the toaster front panel.
The object is explicitly named LED and is positioned with the front controls, making it an indicator light for machine status.
The internal heating elements should become visibly active during toasting, and show/hide behavior is best modeled as an AppearingObject because no animation component is confirmed.

planned_states.name: idle.state
planned_states.description: Toaster is idle and ready for use; carriage is up, heating is off, and indicator is off.

planned_states.name: toasting.state
planned_states.description: Toasting cycle is active; carriage is lowered, heating elements are active, and status indicator is on.

planned_states.name: cancelled.state
planned_states.description: Toasting has been cancelled or finished returning to a non-heating condition; heating is off and indicator is off.

planned_transitions.trigger_description: User lowers the toaster handle to start a toasting cycle.
guard_hints: Handle VALUE > 0.8 to indicate the lever is fully engaged

planned_transitions.trigger_description: User presses the function button to begin a special toasting cycle after setting the controls.
User clicks the stop button to cancel the active toasting cycle.
After the toasting duration elapses, the cycle completes and heating stops.
guard_hints: RotaryButton VALUE > 0.33 and RotaryButton VALUE <= 0.66 → medium timeout

planned_transitions.trigger_description: After the carriage returns and the toaster settles, the system returns to idle.

reasoning: The scene strongly suggests a toaster with a lever, rotary browning knob, function button, stop button, indicator LED, and internal heating elements. I included only objects with clear behavioral roles and excluded structural parts like Body and columns because they do not meaningfully participate in interaction logic. Since no screen assets or animation/particle components are confirmed, I used Light for the LED and AppearingObject for the heating elements, and planned a simple toaster state model of idle, active toasting, and cancelled/completed behavior.
